Issue: Fix multi-axis shear transform to compose individual shear matrices
Decision: Refactor _create_shear function to compose individual single-axis shear matrices rather than directly assigning off-diagonal elements
Rationale: Corrects incorrect multi-axis shear behavior by ensuring shear operations are area-preserving and composable. The 2D implementation builds a rank-3 shear matrix via sequential multiplication of elementary shear matrices, while the 3D implementation composes six elementary shear matrices in a loop.
Author: aymuos15
Description: Shear transform in monai/transforms/utils.py and related test files (tests/transforms/test_create_grid_and_affine.py, tests/transforms/test_rand_affine.py, tests/transforms/test_rand_affined.py)
Risk: Potential side effects or uncertainties introduced by refactoring the shear transform implementation, though not explicitly detailed in the document.

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: The PR refactors the _create_shear function to compose individual single-axis shear matrices rather than directly assigning off-diagonal elements, ensuring area-preserving and composable shear operations.

Argument.author: CodeRabbit Review
Argument.argument: The 2D implementation builds a rank-3 shear matrix via sequential multiplication of elementary shear matrices, while the 3D implementation composes six elementary shear matrices in a loop, correcting incorrect multi-axis shear behavior.

Justification: The decision relates to refactoring a transform function in a utility module (monai/transforms/utils.py) and test files, which is not directly related to ML lifecycle stages like data processing, modeling, or deployment. The focus is on code implementation detail rather than data or model-specific operations.
